meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Fable Gets a Reprieve, Then GPT-5.6 Sol Steals Its Thunder
stories.summary: Anthropic extended Fable 5 access through July 12 right as OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) to the public — kicking off a flood of side-by-side takes. Consensus: Sol isn't quite as "smart" as Fable but is more reliable, determined, and shockingly good at computer use.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text: We're extending access to Claude Fable 5 on all paid plans through July 12.

takes.name: OpenAI
takes.text:
GPT-5.6 Sol, along with Terra and Luna, will launch publicly this Thursday.

We’re expanding preview access globally now. https://t.co/Uk5HcfSc2e

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text:
GPT-5.6 sol launches thursday!

happy building

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
Apparently I'm allowed to talk about GPT-5.6 now?

It's a damn good model. Not quite as "smart" as Fable, but it is incredibly capable. Fixed all the problems I had with GPT-5.5.

It is incredibly determined. Will run for a day without even using a /goal. It understands subagents

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
i've never hyped a model release, we're generally conservative with how we use these things

but gpt-5.6 has had a massive impact on our team, we're using 5x the tokens as we used to

it's not even smarter than fable or anything, but it's just so reliable and fun to use

takes.name: Ben Davis
takes.text:
On Fable being smarter than 5.6 - it is, but:

I think smart is the wrong word, the thing 5.6 is lacking behind fable is the "big model smell" which roughly translates to "understanding the unsaid intent behind your prompt"

fable fills in the blanks better, it writes more

takes.name: Tim
takes.text:
We've been testing GPT-5.6-Sol for over 2 months now. It’s incredibly good in my day-to-day working on Next.js. 

It understands architecture tradeoffs. It can investigate complicated Next.js issue reports. It considers other areas of the codebase when fixing bugs. Needs very

takes.name: Baye
takes.text: Fable 5 不仅是编码能力的提升，Computer Use 能力比 Opus 提升了非常多，真的可以说是全方面的提升了。可惜今晚就要从订阅里移除了。GPT 5.6 应该会无缝跟上，但 5.6 其实已经被小范围测试很久了，从大家的反应平平来看，应该做不了 Fable 的代餐。

takes.name: Dev Ed
takes.text: cmon Anthropic, extend Fable 5 for another week, don't be a lil bitch

takes.name: Gal Shir
takes.text:
It’s over. I’m quitting design.
 
A client of mine just created a logo with Fable 5, and the result left me speechless.
 
It understood the brand story, values, audience, strategy, and turned all of it into a smart, minimal symbol. A genuinely brilliant concept. The kind of idea

takes.name: dex
takes.text: if you are celebrating the fable 5 extension im so confused - everyone i know burned through the allowance before end of the weekend

takes.name: Mckay Wrigley
takes.text: @claudeai my life is currently completely arranged around fable rate limits right now

stories.title: Grok 4.5 Crashes the Party as the Cheap Opus-Class Option
stories.summary: Elon Musk and SpaceXAI shipped Grok 4.5, pitched as Opus-class but faster and cheaper, and it landed instantly in Cursor, Vercel, Warp, and OpenCode. Builders are impressed by the price-to-performance, with Theo calling it "really well priced."

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text:
Based on strong positive feedback from customers in our beta test program, @SpaceXAI will make Grok 4.5 available to the public tomorrow.

It is an Opus-class model, but faster, more token-efficient and lower cost.

takes.name: Michael Truell
takes.text:
Excited to release Grok 4.5 with @SpaceXAI. 

It's an Opus-class model that's fast and low cost. It's a significant step up over any model we've developed so far, including Composer 2.5, and has become the daily driver for many on our team. 

First of many releases. More soon.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
Just realized I was testing Grok 4.5 extensively yesterday lol

Pretty damn good and REALLY well priced

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Grok 4.5 is good enough that I think I have to do a video on it...

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: Grok 4.5 now available to all Vercel customers

takes.name: Ryo Lu
takes.text:
the start of a new era

try Grok 4.5 in Cursor and let us know how it feels!

takes.name: Ben Davis
takes.text:
SpaceXAi overtaking Google to become the third place lab is such a hilarious and sad unforced error from Google

Cannot wait to find out in 5-10 years what actually went so horribly wrong

My current ranking:

1) OpenAI & Anthropic (tie)
3) SpaceXAi
4) zAI
5) Google

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
GLM-5.2 is close to Opus
Grok 4.5 allegedly beats Opus and gpt-5.5
Meta even has something that’s supposedly close to gpt-5.5

Feels like it barely matters now

stories.title: Vercel Scoops Up Better Auth's Bereket to Build Auth for Agents
stories.summary: Vercel announced the Better Auth team (led by Bereket/bekacru) is joining to push open-source auth for apps and agents. The dev community sees it as an inevitable, well-liked move.

takes.name: Vercel
takes.text:
The @better_auth team is joining Vercel to accelerate open source auth for apps and agents.

https://t.co/gboKZoHKz9

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Welcome @bekacru to Vercel. It's a privilege to join forces with Bereket and Better Auth to further our Open SDK vision. 

Developers deserve better auth, to serve humans and agents, built in the open and with taste. That's what Bereket has accomplished. Congrats! 🇪🇹🇺🇸

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
Congrats to my bro @bekacru, if only you knew his story... a straight legend

Congrats to @rauchg @vercel this was a very smart move ♟️ https://t.co/GuMsHtXQ9K

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text: This was inevitable! Congrats to the whole Better Auth team and Vercel.

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text: @rauchg @bekacru hell yeah. love this. love better auth. what a great match.

stories.title: Is Hand-Writing Code Dead? Devs Keep Fighting About It
stories.summary: ThePrimeagen's "1 hour of hand programming, ngmi" jab reignited the debate over how much code devs should still write by hand versus delegating to agents, while Matt Pocock published a guide to stripping the bloat out of Claude Code's system prompt.

takes.name: ThePrimeagen
takes.text:
1 hour of hand programming

ngmi https://t.co/WTw7PIGnJg

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Here's a step-by-step process to kill all the bloat from your Claude Code system prompt:

1. Run a proxy so you can see exactly what gets sent to Claude Code (included in the article)
2. "Fuck, there is so much cruft in there"
3. Use my settings.json to kill all the bloat

Down

takes.name: Mike Futia
takes.text:
I just vibe coded a Meta Ads creative analytics tool in Claude Code 🤯

It plugs into your ad accounts, AI-analyzes every creative you've ever run, and tells you exactly what's working, what isn't, and why.

Built 100% in Claude Code.

Perfect for media buyers and creative https://t.co/ABmhDEeSim

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
vibe coding is coming to an end:

tokens price will increase x10 very soon

stories.title: Design Systems Get an AI Speed-Run
stories.summary: A new tool called HyperDesign that extracts a full design system from any URL into a DESIGN.md file made the rounds, while Vercel's team kept grinding on "boring" design-system refinement and builders swapped notes on their favorite single-purpose apps.

takes.name: Abraham John 🦄🦓
takes.text:
UI/UX Designers, you need to see this.

I just discovered a tool called HyperDesign, and it’s one of the easiest ways to extract a complete design system from any website. Just paste a URL, and it generates a DESIGN.md file with the site’s colors, typography, spacing, and UI https://t.co/5SIO0ATBaJ

takes.name: rauno
takes.text:
this week @merycodes and I are refining the vercel design system by tweaking sizing, alignment, deleting redundancies... doing the "boring work"

why are we doing this? because we care :) https://t.co/OnSxh7LsNo

takes.name: Emil Kowalski
takes.text:
That’s why I also like small apps that do one thing well.

Cleanshot for screenshots.
Eagle for inspiration.
Obsidian for notes.
Things for to-dos.
Cron for calendar.

takes.name: UI/UX Savior
takes.text:
Go-to sites for design inspiration

Web design → https://t.co/vy0wk4V0be
Landing pages → https://t.co/DxjvdQ7FG6
Design systems → https://t.co/mjCv4FbpsX
Animation → https://t.co/LZaaK4dbEA
Apps → https://t.co/NYMEfIFcuG
Brands → https://t.co/mUX72HHuxB https://t.co/12Cdl3uevK

stories.title: Bun's Creator Rewrites It in Rust, Sparks a Language Debate
stories.summary: Jarred Sumner teased rewriting Bun in Rust, and Theo calculated it would've cost ~$165k in API tokens if done that way. Tualatrix framed it as a bigger signal: AI-era tooling favors fully compiled, full-lifecycle languages like Rust.

takes.name: Jarred Sumner
takes.text:
Rewriting Bun in Rust

https://t.co/Rl8bcaxBFc

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: The Bun rewrite in Rust would have cost about $165k in tokens if it was done over API https://t.co/nD88V86Fjc

takes.name: dex
takes.text:
I love the part of the bun rewrite that shows what’s possible and pushes people to ask their models for more

I am concerned about the part where people try to do this for things that 

1) aren’t already designed/architected
2) don’t have such a comprehensive test suite and

takes.name: 图拉鼎
takes.text: 看了这篇 Bun 用 Rust 重写的文章，重点不是 Fable 多厉害，也不是 Rust 多好，而是在 AI 时代，全功能的编译型、全生命周期管理的语言（像 Rust 这样的），将会加速得到适配。Swift，你也要加油啊！

stories.title: Everyone's Racing to Perfect Their Agent "Skills" Playbook
stories.summary: Matt Pocock shipped Skills v1.1 with a full planning-to-review lifecycle (/wayfinder, /to-spec, /implement, /code-review), while other builders debated whether to adopt shared skill libraries or just roll their own.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
mattpocock/skills v1.1 is out!

- /wayfinder helps you plan more ambitious work than ever
- /to-spec and /to-tickets replace /to-prd and /to-issues
- /implement + /code-review complete the whole lifecycle
- /research and /prototype help support wayfinder, or can be used https://t.co/ibrxXXCDb5

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
i don't get using other people's skills

i'll just see what your skill does and make my own

the best part of skills is when you recursively update them

takes.name: Browserbase
takes.text:
Most agents break on the real web when APIs aren't accessible.

Live with @mastra, we'll turn websites into repeatable skills so your browser agents won't ever struggle with the web again.

RSVP below! https://t.co/tW8V65NQIc

stories.title: OpenAI Ships GPT-Live, Its Most Realistic Voice Model Yet
stories.summary: OpenAI rolled out GPT-Live, a next-gen voice model that can search the web and delegate to a frontier model mid-conversation. Early testers say it's spookily realistic — a real step up from prior voice releases.

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text:
GPT-live (next-generation voice) launches today in ChatGPT.

it feels magical and 'real'.

i have always preferred typing to talking to an AI, now i think that's going to shift.

takes.name: OpenAI
takes.text:
The next generation of ChatGPT Voice is here.

Livestream starts at 10am PT.
https://t.co/giS4K1yNh9

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text:
This one was unexpected...

I was not excited for voice models until I tried this one.
It's VERY realistic, almost spooky. 

The coolest part.. the model can search the internet.. fast. And if you've spent any time with GPT-Realtime-2 You know, these voice models are getting very

stories.title: TypeScript 7 Is Officially Out
stories.summary: The TypeScript team shipped TypeScript 7, with a side-by-side `tsc6` package for teams still needing the old API. Kent C. Dodds and others jumped straight to upgrading.

takes.name: TypeScript
takes.text:
📣 The moment is here. 📣

TypeScript 7 is officially released! 7️⃣

https://t.co/D7Mbq7vWpy

takes.name: TypeScript
takes.text:
We know people will still need TS 6 for API usage.

That's why there's a new `@typescript/typescript6` package that provides

- the TypeScript 6 API
- a separate `tsc6` binary

Install it side-by-side with TypeScript 7, and don't worry about conflicts with `tsc`.

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
https://t.co/ua6q1wswqc

TypeScript 7 here we go!

stories.title: Claude Cowork Comes to Mobile and Web
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Cowork on mobile and web, letting you hand off a task at your desk and pick up the finished work from your phone later.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text:
Claude Cowork is coming to mobile and web.

Hand Claude a task at your desk and pick up the finished work from your phone. Close the laptop and Claude keeps going.

Beta is rolling out over the next several weeks starting with the Max plan, with more plans to follow. https://t.co/W4WrgN9TrG

stories.title: A Nostalgic Look Back at Iconic 2000s Websites
stories.summary: Web Design Museum's retrospective posts on the Rockstar Games and Leo Messi official websites from the early 2000s pulled in big engagement — a reminder of how far web design has come.

takes.name: Web Design Museum
takes.text: Rockstar Games website in 2000 https://t.co/OHrAv3hLq2

takes.name: Web Design Museum
takes.text: Leo Messi’s Official Website in 2010 https://t.co/R7dSOIsiNb

hotTakes.name: Theo - t3.gg
hotTakes.text: I'm sorry, I need to crash out about local models for a bit. https://t.co/l4DFC4qWb6

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
hey @OpenAI and @AnthropicAI i have a proposal

your models
but free

how about it?

hotTakes.name: ThePrimeagen
hotTakes.text:
ok I think I have ai pyschosis

reason?

I think its so fun building a harness to play my game that I am not even making much progress on my game.

I think I am a tools dev in my heart and setting up something to run CLI commands / talk to server just touches the fun part of my

hotTakes.name: shadcn
hotTakes.text: Take something we rebuild over and over again, figure out the pattern, abstract it and make it available to everyone.

hotTakes.name: Tanner Linsley
hotTakes.text: Taking a much needed break to welcome my 4th child into this world! See you on the other side! 🎉

hotTakes.name: Micky
hotTakes.text:
SOMEONE LEGIT NEEDS TO CALL THE COPS

DARIO DO SOMETHING MAN https://t.co/KWmS7SOLGW

hotTakes.name: Ben Davis
hotTakes.text:
SpaceXAi overtaking Google to become the third place lab is such a hilarious and sad unforced error from Google

Cannot wait to find out in 5-10 years what actually went so horribly wrong

My current ranking:

1) OpenAI & Anthropic (tie)
3) SpaceXAi
4) zAI
5) Google
